response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with broad experience in web, mobile, and distributed systems, but the role demands deep, current C++ expertise as its core requirement. His C++ exposure is limited to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) and a bachelor thesis project; C++ does not even appear in his listed skills. His primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js/web technologies, and there is no evidence of multi-threaded systems programming, MQTT, industrial protocols, IEC 62443 compliance, or industrial automation domain knowledge — all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Strong background in event-driven and micro-services architectures, including a high-throughput system handling millions of HTTP requests per second at Dunnhumby.
Demonstrated ability to work across multiple layers of the stack (backend, frontend, embedded, DevOps) and adopt new tools pragmatically.
Solid foundation in software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, code mentoring) that align with the role's quality and mentorship expectations.
concerns: C++ proficiency is essentially absent from active experience — last used over a decade ago for ~6 months; not listed as a current skill.
No exposure to industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), Secure Development Lifecycle practices, or industrial communication protocols (MQTT, OPC UA, Modbus, IEC 61850).
No background in automation machinery, IoT platforms, or embedded/real-time systems engineering relevant to the target industry.
